prose: **Ferrocene Fukui reactivity descriptors** — GFN2-xTB (semi-empirical tight-binding, xtb program), gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1. Neutral state charges computed via Mulliken partitioning. Cation (N−1) and anion (N+1) states at multiplicities 2 (doublet). Fukui functions f⁺, f⁻, and dual descriptor (f⁺ − f⁻) computed from charge differences. Both charge conservation checks passed: Σ(f⁺) = 1.0000, Σ(f⁻) = 1.0000, indicating N±1 state convergence. No warnings. **Most reactive sites:** Iron (atom 11) is the most electrophilic site (f⁺ = 0.22885456) and most nucleophilic site (f⁻ = 0.25938191); dual descriptor = −0.030527354 (negative, favoring nucleophilic reactivity). Hydrogen atoms show elevated Fukui indices (~0.063) vs. carbon atoms (~0.014), reflecting relative electronegativity in the cyclopentadienyl ligands. Visualization plot saved to the calculation directory. Integrity: trustworthy = true; all checks passed. CLI: `chemkit fukui --method xtb --charge 0 --mult 1 /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/ferrocene/ferrocene.xyz`
